programmes.show_description: How do they build one of the world's most powerful locomotives? And how do they turn cows' milk into clotted cream?
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. Looking at sardine fishing, Piaggio scooters and erector sets.
Carl's Fiesta RS Turbo. Carl would love to own a classic Fiesta RS Turbo, but he spends all his spare cash on his family. Mike takes big gamble to secure this dream car.
Kerry's Nissan Patrol. The guys try to hunt down a classic Nissan Patrol for surfer Kerry. She hands over her daily motor, a VW Touran, which breaks down en route to the garage.
Stumpie's American Take Away. In the New Forest, Hampshire, Henry and Sam visit a collector of Americana. What rare bicycles and classic sportsters will they find in his sheds?
Chiller Thriller. In the Midlands, Henry and Sam visit the sheds of a bike builder. He has a collection of rare bikes, but it's the surprise in his freezer that attracts the duo.
Follow the production process of hearing aids, 3D puzzles and toilets, and find out how each of these items is made.
Follow the production process of copy paper, jeans and computers, and find out how each of these items is made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Mark uncovers an 80-year-old radio with a notable history. Meanwhile, Suzie restores a beloved games kit and Steve works on a grandfather clock with sentimental value.
Masters Of The Trade. On a trip from Manchester to Hampshire, Drew visits expert dealers, snapping up a campaign chair, a Georgian bookcase, and a 19th-century carved deer.
In The Family. Drew seeks out a combination of knowledge and experience when he visits four family businesses. He discovers an exciting array of items to sell on.
The Scrappers launch their season determined to find a huge gold haul. Meanwhile, new crew the Victoria Diggers may have stumbled on a million-dollar motherlode.
One team breaks all records, unearthing the biggest gold nugget ever found on television. Neville and Mick are hampered by a run-in with one of the deadliest snakes on the planet.
On Tim and Irita's second trip, Irita's on top and in the Suffolk pink, while he breaks down before giving her a fright. Will bidders love the sweetheart brooch?
Angus and the team are in Derbyshire to help a widow clear out her home. They uncover a riding crop, a 19th century stool and a jewellery collection.
Mark uncovers an 80-year-old radio with a notable history. Meanwhile, Suzie restores a beloved games kit and Steve works on a grandfather clock with sentimental value.
The Need For Speed. Henry and Sam are in Lancashire, visiting a petrol head with a collection of vintage bikes and rare British and American four-wheelers.
Bicycles And Stationary Engines. In Oxfordshire, Henry and Sam find a shed full of different items. Sam has his eye on some model trains while Henry finds a vintage bike.
Nir And Olivia. Henry and Fuzz check out Nir's collection of vehicles, including American air taxis, 1920s Citroen saloons and 1980s Japanese Superbikes.
Norman. Henry and Fuzz meet Norman, a lifetime petrolhead. His shed is filled with vintage cars, film props, bikes, and classic Mercedes saloons.
Angus helps Craig Revel Horwood auction off items from his glamorous house. Later, the team explore the home of legendary broadcaster John Sergeant.
Brian enlists Angus to help his singer daughter Lucy raise money for a mixing desk. And Angus visits Edwina's home to help raise funds for Ukraine.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Dino / Morgan Sports Car. Henry and Fuzz are in West Sussex at Dino's mega-shed. He's got buses, vintage sports cars, rare motorbikes and oodles of motoring ephemera.
Chopper Roy / Triton And Bantam. Henry and Fuzz head to 'Chopper Roy's' place. His vintage British motorbikes and a classic seventies sports coupé catch their eye.
Old Mates And Motor-Racing. Richard gets into hot water as he tries to upgrade his racing licence and the Cogs head to the Isle of Man TT to help launch a new British motorbike.
Henry tackles his biggest project, restoring a Bell 47 helicopter for the 80th Anniversary Flight. It's a race against time to get it off the ground.
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. Featuring Titan Tires, espresso and how diamonds are cut.
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. Featuring the complex network of Paris sewers, Beretta's and cotton manufacture.
David's VW Golf GTI. Mike and Elvis help teacher David hunt down his dream car, a classic VW Golf GTI. But things get off to a rocky start when they have to scrap David's van.
Mo's Porsche Cayman. Mike and Elvis help 51-year-old Mo who's been trying to save for a Porsche Cayman ever since he started working. Can the boys turn Mo's dreams into reality?
The Need For Speed. Henry and Sam are in Lancashire, visiting a petrol head with a collection of vintage bikes and rare British and American four-wheelers.
Bicycles And Stationary Engines. In Oxfordshire, Henry and Sam find a shed full of different items. Sam has his eye on some model trains while Henry finds a vintage bike.
Follow the production process of yoghurts, candles and neon signs, and find out how each of these items is made.
Follow the production process of bottle tops, chocolate, pills and pasta, and find out how each of these items is made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Lucia uses an unusual painting method from Michelangelo's era in a restoration. Meanwhile, Steve and Will tackle a table with a secret cocktail compartment.
Georgian Special. Drew indulges his Georgian obsession in Lincoln, where he falls for a Regency wine cooler. And a bungalow near Stockport hides a vast collection.
Survivors. Drew finds rare pieces that have stood the test of time. They include an 18th century cabinet in superb original condition and a chest from the Vatican.
The Gypsies strike a patch of gold but are distracted when thieves raid their nearby lease. Meanwhile, torrential rain disrupts production for the Dirt Dogs.
The Victoria Diggers' million-dollar dream is nearly at breaking point. Plus, the Dirt Dogs hunt down gold thieves and the Mahoneys stumble across a mysterious murder.
On a journey through Bedfordshire that includes airships and tractors, Irita splurges on fine silver while Tim goes for bargain basement. Who'll clean up at auction?
In County Durham, Angus and the team clear out a huge aviation collection. Later, they help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home.
Lucia uses an unusual painting method from Michelangelo's era in a restoration. Meanwhile, Steve and Will tackle a table with a secret cocktail compartment.
Ploughman's. Henry and Sam rummage through a tractor enthusiasts heaven, sheds and fields full of old machines. Their attention is caught by an old vintage plough.
Army Trailers And Trinkets. Henry and Sam find themselves down in the South West rummaging around another belting shed. There are so many things on offer they find it hard to choose.
Stephen. Henry and Fuzz visit Stephen, a diehard biker with a shed full of rare Czech and Japanese bikes, vintage cans, and motoring curios.
Graham Steeler. Henry and Fuzz head to Southend to visit Graham's shed full of bikes and muscle cars. They find a Dutton kit car and rows and rows of rare bikes.
Jon Culshaw entertains Angus with hilarious impressions as they dig through clutter. Plus, actress Catherine Tyldesley auctions off her jewellery.
Angus helps Claire Sweeney sell off some very glamorous items. Plus, Christopher Biggins needs to declutter - can he part with any of his beloved art collection?
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
The Old Master And The Model Maker. Angus discovers a mysterious 17th century artwork in Scotland. Then, he heads to Peterborough to help clear the home of a keen model maker.
Angus helps the ex-EastEnders star Samantha Womack shift her showbiz things, and former MP Alan Johnson parts with a chess set and much-loved guitar.
Hi-Do-Hi! and panto star Jeffrey Holland bids farewell to treasures, while broadcaster Kaye Adams makes headlines with her rare Danish furniture.
What is the secret of the lion dance, how are tunnels built, and what is a super sopper? Find out as more items are put under the microscope.
How do they make pistachios, what is the Forbidden City, and how do they produce radars? Find out as more items are put under the microscope.
